sections.paragraph:
ἀναισθητέω (αἰσθάνομαι ‘perceive by the senses’; Demosth. 18, 221; Plut., Mor. 1103d; 1105a; TestLevi 3:10; Philo, Ebr. 6; 154; Ath. 15, 3, R. 78, 2) to be without feeling, be unfeeling, insensible τινός toward something (Plut., Mor. 1062c; Jos., Bell. 4, 165, Ant. 11, 176; Ath. 15, 3) τῆς χρηστότητος αὐτοῦ having no feeling for his goodness IMg 10:1. Abs. (Epicurus, Ep. 1 p. 59, 6 [p. 21 Us.]) lack perception Dg 2:8f.

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 64
